characters.name: "Mini Mart" Marv
characters.description: "Mini Mart" Marvwas a character who appears in the Season 7 episode ofNight Courttitled"Attack of the Mac Snacks". The part of Mini Mart Marv is played in the episode in played byCliff Emmich. Mini Mart Marv, a man that owned a national chain of  4,000 stores calledMarv's Metro Mini Marts, was invited byHarryto try someMac Snacks, which were a delicacy created byMac's wife Quon Le, hoping that he would stock them in his mini marts. Unfortunately, Marv died right after eating some Mac Snacks, causing a panic because people suspected the snacks were poisonous. It turns out that Marv had a heart attack because his arteries were clogged with Marshmallow Prune Gooey.

characters.name: Al Craven
characters.description: Al Cravenis a recurring character onNight Courtwho appears in four episodes in the series's first season,"The Former Harry Stone","Harry and the Rockstar","Pick a Number", and"Harry and the Madam". Al is played in the episodes byTerry Kiser. Al Craven, a newspaper reporter forThe Manhattan Gazette, is quite snoopy. In trying to land that "big scoop" of a story, he tends to follow leads, often without checking them over thoroughly, particuarly if they might lead to a scoop on a scandalous story. In his appearance in the episode "The Former Harry Stone", his first, he overhears thatJudge Stonehas a criminal record and presses the matter until Harry admits that he once drove off with his dad's car and crashed into the front of a liquor store. Craven didn't think the story was scandalous enough and chose not to print it as he doesn't see anything interesting in"a judge who once got busted for taking a teenage joyride"!

characters.name: Alan Kitch
characters.description: Alan Kitchappears in the episode ofNight CourttitledChristine and Mac (a.k.a Daddy for the Defense)"in Season 2. The part of Alan is played byJay Gerber, who also appears asMr. Lollerin"Dad's First Date"in Season 3, then asSydney Shoupin"Author, Author"in Season 4. WhenChristine Sullivanmakes her firstNight Courtappearance as a fill in forBillie Young, who took the night off, she endures an impromptu greeting in the courthouse cafeteria from a man in a shirt, slacks, tie and expensive looking overcoat; thinking that it was a fellow lawyer, she gets greeted by the man. As she introduces herself to the man answers back"It's gonna be a pleasure working with you!"When she answers,"Are you a lawyer?", the man, which happens to Alan, who is in court on an indecent exposure charge, responds"No, I'm a criminal! Indecent exposure!"

characters.name: Albert "Al" Slotkin
characters.description: Albert "Al" Slotkinwas a character who appears in the Season 3 epiosde ofNight Court, titled"The Hostage". The part of Mr. Slotkin is played in the episode byKenneth Tigar, who later appears in the series asGus Melmanin the episode"Her Honor: Part 4"in Season 5. Mr. Slotkin being presented by prosecutor Dan. Mr. Slotkin, a strange man who appeared in the episode"The Hostage", was brought into court after being charged with stealing electronic components in trying to communicate with the planet "Saturn". Slotkin, who really hails from New Jersey, winds up holding the court hostage with a handgun stolen from court custody which belonged to a group ofsubway commuterswho pulled them on a French tourist asking for change for a dollar! At first threating to take the stenographer)Charlotte Portney), Plotkin takes Dan hostage and demands the rest of the parts he needs to communicate with his home planet of "Saturn"; as well as the resources, such as plutonium, which he said could be substituted by SPAM, nitrogen, and hydrogen.

characters.name: Amanda Caswell
characters.description: Amanda Caswellappears in the episode ofNight Courttitled"For Love or Money"in Season 7. The part of Amanda is played by stage/TV actressKaren Morrow. Buddy and wealthy widow Amanda Caswell before their "nuptials" in the episode "For Love of Money" in season 7. In the Season 7 episode"For Love and Money"Buddy Ryan,Harry'sdad, begin starts dating Amanda, who's a wealthy millionaire's widow, after she is brought into Harry's courtroom after her limousene hit a chestnut stand, as she was rushing to try to make a charity event. After Anita agrees to pay the chestnut stand owner, aMr. Poplinsky(Bill Capizzi), the amount of the damages to his truck, which was $5,000, she and Buddy, who just got a new job at a restaurant nearby, happen to meet and hit it off.

characters.name: Angela Korchak
characters.description: Angela Korchakwas a character who appears in the Season 2 episode of Night Court titled"Harry and the Madam". The part of Angela is played in the episode byLinda Hart. In the"Harry and the Madam"episode, one of New York City's oldest and most notoriously known alleged madams,Irene Danbury, who runs one of the city's best kwown brothels, and who is also a well respected socialite who's known to donate large amounts of money to various charitable orgaizations and the arts, is brought into court for prosecution on a soliciation charge stemming from a police raid on her brothel from a tip from hooker Angela, who was one of her former workers, and who was testifying against her after being fired for stealing tips from the johns. When Dan, who represented her in her case against Madame Irene, is hugged by Angela after Judge Stone ruled in favor of her case against Irene, Dan becomes "anal-rententive" in dealing with her; in not wanting to be "infected" with any STD's, he adamantly tells her"do not touch me until you've received a note from a doctor!"

characters.name: Angela, girlfriend of Dan
characters.description: Angelawas a character who appeared in the Season 2 episode ofNight Courttitled"Married Alive". The part of Angela was played byAngela Aames, who also appeared as another character namedAngela, the nudist, in the episode"Dad's First Date", asDebbiein the episode"Earthquake", then asUrsulain"Rabid"in Season 4. In the episode"Married Alive", when Dan begins datingPatty Douglas, a wealthy heiress to her father, a wealthy business entrepenuer and mogul with questionable, possibly criminal ties who doesn't approve of the two getting engaged and possibly married, Patty, feeling pressure from her dad, decides not to go through with their plans together, and breaks the engagement in the cafeteria. Feeling dejected, he happens to run into Angela, a paralegal, one of his former dates, who works in the courthouse. They manage to arrange a date together for that Friday, as Dan recovers from losing what was the only woman he fell for on the series.

characters.name: Angela, the nudist
characters.description: Angela, a nudist, appears in the episode ofNight Courttitled"Dad's First Date"inSeason 3. The part of Angela is played byAngela Aames, who also appears asAngela, a former date of Dan'sin the Season 2 episodeMarried Alive"and also asDebbiein"Earthquake", then asUrsulain"Rabid"in Season 4. Angela, fully clothed, after getting disrobed in Harry's chambers, says she felt "exhiliarted" when Dan got her to exercise, disrobed in "Dad's First Date" in Season 2. When Angela and the rest of the nudist camp which she's a part of are all hauled into court on indecent exposure charges, amid complaints from residents who live near the camp in the episode"Dad's First Date", They all assert their constituional right to go nude, as they don't believe in wearing clothing when in the privacy of their own colony. As Judge Stone gathers them all into his chambers to talk the issue over, they disrobe and put on towels; However, Dan, ever the one to ogle a sexy looking chick, manaes to get the sexy looking Angela to run in place, apparently with the towel off, while in Harry's chambers, getting his proverbial "rocks" off!

characters.name: Angry Man with Eyepatch
characters.description: The Angry Man with Eyepatchwas a minor character who appears in theSeason 2episode (#4) ofNight Courttitled"Pick a Number". The part of the character was played in the episode by comedian/actor and comedy writerDon Sherman. The angry man with an eyepatch was just one of many people who floodedJudge Harry Stone'scourtroom pretending to be needy in trying to earn his sympathy after word was leaked of him being entrusted by a humble manLeo Baldassare(played bySydney Lassick) to try to find someone who was needy enough to receve the $3,00,000 prize form the winning NY state Lottery ticket that he has in his possession. The angry gets into shouting match with a man,Jerome Chapel(Ralph Manza) who entered the courtroom in a hospital gown with a an IV pretending to be sick, argues to the man that he was so poor as a child that he had"rabbit dolls for pets"as he then argued back that his father"gave him pet rocks and told him to name them!"

characters.name: Aphrodite
characters.description: Aphroditewas a minor character who appeared in theSeason 2episode of titled"Christine and Mac (a.k.a. Daddy for the Defense)"(#2). The part of Aphrodite was played byTessa Richarde, who also appears as a hooker inseason 3's"The Hostage"(episode #2) , and then asRoberta DeVriesin"Earthquake"inSeason 4(episode #9). When Aphrodite, a hooker brought in for solicitation, observes the courtroom proceedings while awaiting her case, along withChristine Sullivan, the new temporary (later the permanent one inSeason 3) get her objection to a motion in the case of a oneKeith Landon(Paul Lieber), who was charged with criminal mischief, overruled byJudge Stone, as Christine's dadJack(Eugene Roche), who takes offense, also observing the proceedings, says to her"He can't talk to my daughter like that!", Aprhrodite replies,"He's a judge, honey... In his courtroom, he can abuse anybody and force them to do whatever it is he wants...For free!" Read more:https://www.springfieldspringfield.co.uk/view_episode_scripts.php?tv-show=night-court-1984&episode=s02e02"

characters.name: Arnold Preminger
characters.description: Arnold Premingerwas a character who appeared in the Season 3 episode ofNight Courttitled"Dan's Boss". The part of Arnold is played in the episode byNightmare on Elm Streethorror/slasher film series starRobert Englund. Arnold Preminger, a defendant who was brought into court for attempted arson, as he thinks, as the case is presented by Dan before the bench, that "the Earth is being invaded by aliens from the Gaseous-Nebula Gamma 7 were infiltrating the municipal work force in their constant, continued quest for universal domination!". and they should fight back. When Preminger says that he has proof, from a New York Times newspaper article, Harry corrects him saying "Mr. Premminger, this is an article about immigrants!"

characters.name: Art Fensterman
characters.description: Art Fenstermanis a recurring character who appears in a total of 37 episodes ofNight Court, beginning with the season 1 episode"Some Like it Hot"; his final appearance is in the two-episode, series ending story"Opportunity Knocks". Art is played byMike Finneran. Art is a maintennace worker at the Manhattan Criminal Court building. His duties include spraying for pests, elevator maintenance, and other general maintenance duties. A rather bumbling "fix-it-man", his attempts to fix things that go wrong in the courthouse building often disrupts Harry's courtroom proceedings.

characters.name: Leo Baldassare
characters.description: Leo Baldassarewas a chartacter who appeared onNight Courtin theSeason 2episode"Pick a Number"(episode #4). The part of Leo is played in the episode by veteran TV/film character actorSydney Lassick. Leo, who's a quite humble man, and who claims that he is life is "quite full" and that he's "already happy", asks and enlistsJudge Stoneto try to find someone who needs the $3,000,000 he's just won in the New York Lottery, as he has the winning ticket in his possesion. When word begins to leak out to the public about Harry's task to award a needy prospective recipient of the winning ticket, his courtroom becomes inundated with all types liars and huckster types, all vying to cash in on Leo's "benevolence", as Harry decides that the humble Leo is the most worhty to receive the money, as Leo then decides, of all things, to give it all to a playwright namedArnie Prince(Bobby Ramsen) as financial backing for a Broadway musical titled, of all titles,"Mussolini: A Man and his Music"!

characters.name: Alice Beeker
characters.description:
Alice Beeker, who runs a sperm clinic, is a character who appears in the episodeMurderinSeason 4ofNight Court(episode #12). The part of Alice is played by actressAlice Drummond, who previously made a guest appearance in the series asMavis Tuttle, a replacement court clerk, in theSeason 1episode"Harry and the Rockstar"(episode #11). Alice Beeker is vice president of the Greater Manhattan (NY) Sperm Bank, in charge of "candidate selection". When Alice, who appears in the court as a representing the sperm bank concerning a defendant who attacked his mailman after he and his wife opened a package of a "sample" which was errantly delivered to his address instead of the sperm bank, she asks Dan if he would like to be a donor; when he says to her "Who would degrade oneself" by donating, she tells him he could get up to $100 a shot (for 3 times a week, as required), which makes him change his mind about donating as he then says "I could degrade myself into a Porsche by the end of the year!", as she responds,"Zoom, Zoom, Zoom!" The next day she drops by the courtroom again to informDanthat his sperm count was too low for him to be considered a suitable donor, he goes ballistic. She the asks him,"when wasthe last time you had sex?"as he then as her"What time is it?", as she advises him to abstain from sex for a couple of weeks before he makes another visit to the sperm bank in hopes that his count will then be higher.
When she later drops by the courtroom cafetria to inform him that he clinic made a mistake in the analysis of his sperm count, she tells him "turns out we made a mistake...it was a new analyis section" who had switched his sperm sample with that of another donor, as she says to him "your sperm is just dandy!"  
Now, an angry, even more agitated Dan, who hasn't had sex in 2 weeks, responds,"Just like that !!?? Wham, bam, thank you, Dan ??!!"When Dan, who then broods to himself"It's 3:00 in the morning, and I don't have a date!"Judge Stone warns all the women to"Run for your lives!"...and then, the men! As Dan contemplates his predicament, Alice enters wearing an evening gown and warns Dan"I'll be down in the car...don't keep me waiting!", apparently for an "assignation" in her car in the parking garage!

characters.name: Bent Barbara
characters.description: "Bent Barbara" was a pseudonym for an elderly woman who appeared in court on a sex violation. She, along with a small group of colleagues, ran an unregistered phone sex business. While explaining to the court that their business was quite successful despite their age, Barbara revealed that their clients were the type to share their deepest sexual fantasies. After a short demonstration where she revealed her fake name, a look of utter horror crossed Dan Fielding's face. Upon hearing his panicked voice, Barbara and her coworker recognized him as a regular customer who went by the name "Vic Vaselino". Fielding quickly tried to cover up by affecting a Scottish brogue, however Barbara was not fooled. After a few seconds, she turned to Christine Sullivan and announced that she'd been included in many of Dan's calls to the service.

characters.name: Bernie
characters.description: Bernieappeared in four episodes of Night Court; the Season 1 episodes"Hi Honey, I'm HomeandWelcome Back, Momma", and the Season 2 episodes"Billie's Valentine"and"Dan's Boss". The part of Bernie is played in the episodes byMartin Garner. Bernie was the guy who owned the news stand in the cafeteria. He relentlessly pursuedSelmawho at first, did not reciprocated his feelings for her. Eventually, Selma would warm up to Bernie and become "good friends" with him, as long as he kept his amorous "urges" in check. By the timeFlostarts working there in Season 3, Bull warns her about how he pestered Selma and so Flo countered by coming on twice as strong towards him and frightened him away.

characters.name: Biff
characters.description: Biff, confirming Mr.Douglas's threat to Dan as geniune, in the episode "Married, Alive" in Season 2. Biff,the goon-looking driver/courier for aMr. Douglas, the wealthy business mogul dad ofPatty Douglas, who briefly becomes engaged toDan Fielding, appears in the Season 2 episode"Married Alive". The part of Biff is played in the episode by B-movie/TV character actorPatrick Wright. In the episode"Married Alive", when Dan begins dating Patty, whose father is wealthy business magnate with questionable, possibly organized crime ties, he begins her, for what Harry and the others thought was because she, an heiress to her dad's fortune, stands to inherit up to $40 million. But, as they begin dating, the two really hit it off, surprisingly, as they get engaged, and contemplate marrying. Whe Mr.Douglas's, who's quite overprotective of his daughter, offers to give Dan $50,000 to stop dating her, gets turned down, he, who apparently has criminal ties as well, tells Dan "If I find out that you've slept with her (Patty), it's soprano city!", as his driver/vailet and goon Biff says in very high pitched voice, which belies his very hulking presence"And he means it, too!"

characters.name: Bill Oxley
characters.description: Bill Oxleywas a character who appeared in the Season 5 episode ofNight Courttitled"Who Was That Masked Man?". The part of the Oxley was played in the episode byMatthew Laurance. In the episode"Who was that Masked Man?", when a Geoff Prescott, who played by the part ofThe Red Rangera popular character in the 1950's through the 1980's on the widely popular "The Saturday Matinee" TV show is brought into court for getting into a scuffle with Oxley, who worked for the studio in whichThe Red Rangercharacter was produced, when Oxley tried to take off his mask, even after the studio had obtained a court order barring him from appearing as the character in public, this while the film studio is premiering a "New Adventures of The Red Ranger" film, with a new actor, which knaws at Prescott, who originated the character. As the Ranger, or, Prescott, and Oxley are presented in court, he still refuses to take off his mask, he admits that he never took of his mask in 42 years. Oxley, who refers to Prescott, as "a washed up old geezer", he maintains the studio's rights to the character and that Prescott is breaking the law in wearing the Ranger mask in public.

characters.name: Billie Young
characters.description: Billie Youngwas a public defender at the Manhattan Criminal Court onNight Court. The part of Billie was played by Singer/ActressEllen Foley. Billie, is, at heart, a very good-natured soul, but quite feisty, especially when it comes to defending her clients; she's quite a dedicated attorney. Good-natured, Billie, like Harry, has quite a witty sense of light hearted humor about her. She served as a friend and potential love interest for Harry in Season 2 of the series. Ellen Foley, who plays Billie on the series, was the original female vocal on the song"Paradise By The Dashboard Light"with Meat Loaf. Foley has released several albums under her name as well as being a member of the all female group "Pandora's Box."

characters.name: Bob Wheeler
characters.description: Bob Wheeleris a character who appears in six episodes ofNight Court, beginning with the 2-episode story titled"The Wheels of Justice (Part 1)"and"The Wheels of Justice (Part 2)". The part of Bob Wheeler is played byBrent Spiner, who would go on to play the part of Lt. Commander Data onStar Trek: The Next Generation. Bob Wheeler and his wifeJune Wheelerare poor, unfortunate people. Every time they appear beforeJudge Stonethey have an extremely pathetic story. They seem to have the worst luck at every turn. At one point, they use all of their money to purchase the news stand in the cafeteria of the Manhattan criminal court building. Because they were extremely overcharged for the news stand, they were required to sell all of their items at an enormous mark-up.

characters.name: Brent
characters.description: Brent, a male friend ofSelma Hacker, appears in the Season 2 episode ofNight Courttitled"An Old Flame". The part of Brent is played in the episode byDaniel Greene. In Brent's only appearance in"An Old Flame", when Selma declines an offer from old flameMarty Ratnerto rekindle their one-time romance and move to Florida to live with him, she runs into male friend Brent, who's much younger than she, as he asks her if she would like to go out with him that Friday night, as she, who apparently has no hangups about dating much younger guys, accepts!

characters.name: Oscar Brown
characters.description: Gilbert Gottfried as temporary prosecuting attorney Oscar Brown in "A Guy Named Phantom", the Season 1 opening episode. Oscar Brownwas a temporary replacement court attorney forDan Fieldingafter he went missing in theSeason 9opening episode"A Guy Named Phantom (Part 1)"; he would also appear in the second part of the season opening story,"A Guy Named Phantom (Part 2)"and the episode"Looking for Mr. Shannon"(#7) that season. The part of Oscar is played in the episodes by comedian/actorGilbert Gottfried. A rather quite wacky sort, Oscar, who filled in for the absent Dan, who went into hiding after the funds of the Phil Foundation, all 11 million dollars, was pilfered from its bank account, as Oscar is quite overzealous in his argument concerning a jaywalker, Father Fitzgerald, who's a priest, who he calls "a menace to society", is found guilty and charged $10, as he says the father is "guilty, guilty, guilty, and should be removed from the streets!" When Judge Stone reminds Oscar that the defendant is only guilty of jaywalking, and not "a menace to society", in awarding the defendant's fine with time served, Oscar gleefully chimes, "Yet another win for the prosecution!" as he says its "like shooting fish in a barrel!"

characters.name: Buddy Ryan
characters.description: Buddy Ryanappears in ten episodes ofNight Courtfrom seasons 4 to 9. The part of Buddy is played by comedian/actorJohn Astin. Buddy, who's a semi-retired circus performer, and somewhat of a magician like Harry is the Judge's biological father. At first, he tells Harry that he is his step-father, but later he reveals that he is his real father. Buddy spent a good deal of time in and out of multiple mental hospitals, where he met Harry's late mother, who was also hospitalized there before she married (and then divorced} Buddy, as Harry was adopted by the man who raised him, Harry Stone, Sr. and his wife. In the Season 4 episode"The Next Voice You Hear...", his first appearance, Harry learns he was conceived in a mental hospital, as he then asks Buddy"I'm gonna go out on a limb here... you weren't on the staff, were you?", as Buddy smiles and shakes his head and responds"But I'm feeling much better now!"

characters.name: Bull Shannon
characters.description: Aristotle Nostradamus "Bull" Shannonis one of the bailiffs at the Manhattan Municipal Criminal Court on theNight CourtTV series. The part of Bull is played by veteran character/voice actorRichard Moll. A (seemingly) dim-witted hulk of a figure, Bull, at heart is actually gentle and often childlike, with a certain honest naivete about him. He was fiercely protective of Harry. Bull would also choose a series of words every month from the dictionary and try to put them in a sentence. Bull was known for his catchphrase, "Ohh-kay," and clapping a hand loudly to his forehead when he realized he had made a mistake. His other trademark trait was when he would become upset he would moan a low pitch whine that became a loud wail as he stormed off in anger or sadness. Bull was especially close to fellow bailiffSelma Hacker, who acted as a mother figure to the tough-looking but childlike Shannon. In the Season 3 opening episode"Hello, Goodbye", Bull is distraught upon hearing of Selma's death: he goes on an all-night bender with a group of circus performers and shows up the next day drunk-and-disorderly. After talking toHarry, Bull is eventually is able to come to grips with Selma's passing.

characters.name: Arnold Burger
characters.description: Arnold Burgerwas a character who appeared in onNight Court, in theSeason 2opening episode"The Nun". The part of Arnold is played in the episode byEarl Boen. When Arnold who pressed charges againtAnita Fries(played byRandee Heller), a hooker, on trial charged with solicitation of prostitution, robbery and assault on him (in a funny sequence, Bull announces the case "Burger & Fries!" like a fast food order) , is called to the bench with to plead his  case, he accused her of stealing his wallet, as he also said that "and she refused to give it back!" Arnold pleading his case against Anita

characters.name: Louise Cahill
characters.description: U.S. Rep. Louise Cahillwas a recurring character who appeared onNight Courtin theSeason 9episodesParty Girl (Part 1)andParty Girl (Part 2)(episodes #16, 17). The part of Congresswoman Cahill was played by veteran TV/film actressNancy Marchand. Congresswoman Cahill promises Christine, who's running against her in the upcoming primary, the fight of her life, and to "annihiate her" as she's done against her opponents in the previous 10 elections. When a political consultant namedJohhny Dugan(Don Amendolia) persuades Christine to run for Congress, as he persuades her that she's a younger, fresh political voice who needs to be heard as she the prepares to run against the incumbent, longtime veteran U.S. representative Louise Cahill, whom, ironically, Dugan worked for for many years as her advisor, as the campaign attacks starts to get slanerdously vicious on both sides.
